methods.name: splunktalib.timer_queue.TimerQueue.start
documentation: Start the timer queue to make it start function

documentation: Remove timer from the queue.
A timer queue implementation, runs a separate thread to handle timers

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.property_endpoints.create_properties
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param conf_name: the name of the conf file, e.g. "props"
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
param stanza: stanza name, e.g. "aws:cloudtrail"
return: None on success else raise exception
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.property_endpoints.get_property
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param conf_name: the name of the conf file, e.g. "props"
param key: the property name
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
param stanza: stanza name, e.g. "aws:cloudtrail"
return: the property value
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.property_endpoints.update_properties
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param conf_name: the name of the conf file, e.g. "props"
param key_values: the key-value dict of the stanza
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
param stanza: stanza name, e.g. "aws:cloudtrail"
return: raise exception when failed
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.data_input_endpoints.reload_data_input
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param input_type: |-
  name of the input type.
  if it is a script input, the input is "script",
  for modinput, say snow, the input is "snow"
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.data_input_endpoints.create_data_input
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param input_type: |-
  name of the input type.
  if it is a script input, the input is "script",
  for modinput, say snow, the input is "snow"
param key_values: a K-V dict of details in the data input stanza.
param name: |-
  The name of the input stanza to create.
  i.e. stanza [\<input_type>://\<name>] will be created.
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
return: None on success else raise exception
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.data_input_endpoints.get_data_input
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param input_type: |-
  name of the input type.
  if it is a script input, the input is "script",
  for modinput, say snow, the input is "snow"
param name: |-
  The name of the input stanza to create.
  i.e. stanza [\<input_type>://\<name>] will be deleted.
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
return: a list of stanzas in the input type, including metadata
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.data_input_endpoints.update_data_input
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param input_type: |-
  name of the input type.
  if it is a script input, the input is "script",
  for modinput, say snow, the input is "snow"
param key_values: a K-V dict of details in the data input stanza.
param name: |-
  The name of the input stanza to create.
  i.e. stanza [\<input_type>://\<name>] will be updated.
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
return: raise exception when failure
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.data_input_endpoints.delete_data_input
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param input_type: |-
  name of the input type.
  if it is a script input, the input is "script",
  for modinput, say snow, the input is "snow"
param name: |-
  The name of the input stanza to create.
  i.e. stanza [\<input_type>://\<name>] will be deleted.
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
---

\:return raise exception when failed

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.data_input_endpoints.operate_data_input
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param input_type: |-
  name of the input type.
  if it is a script input, the input is "script",
  for modinput, say snow, the input is "snow"
param name: |-
  The name of the input stanza to create.
  i.e. stanza [\<input_type>://\<name>] will be operated.
param operation: must be "disable" or "enable"
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
---

methods.name: splunktalib.kv_client.KVClient.create_collection
documentation:
---
collection: collection name
return: None if successful otherwise KV exception thrown
---

methods.name: splunktalib.kv_client.KVClient.list_collection
documentation:
---
collection: collection name. When euqals "None", return all
---

collections in the system.
\:return: a list containing the connection names if successful, throws
KVNotExists if no such colection or other exception if other error
happened

methods.name: splunktalib.kv_client.KVClient.delete_collection
documentation:
---
collection: collection name to be deleted
return: None if successful otherwise throw KVNotExists exception if
---

the collection doesn't exist in the system or other exception if other
error happened

methods.name: splunktalib.kv_client.KVClient.insert_collection_data
documentation:
---
collection: collection name
data: dict like key values to be inserted and attached to
---

this collection
\:return: {"\_key": "key_id"} when successful, clients can use this
key to do query/delete/update, throws KV exceptions when failed

methods.name: splunktalib.kv_client.KVClient.delete_collection_data
documentation:
---
collection: collection name
key_id: key id returned when creation. If None, delete all data
---

associated with this collection
\:return: None if successful otherwise throws KV exception

methods.name: splunktalib.kv_client.KVClient.update_collection_data
documentation:
---
collection: collection name
key_id: key id returned when creation
return: key id if successful otherwise throws KV exception
---

methods.name: splunktalib.kv_client.KVClient.get_collection_data
documentation:
---
collection: collection name
key_id: key id returned when creation. If None, get all data
---

associated with this collection
\:return: when key_id is not None, return key values if
successful. when key_id is None, return a list of key values if
sucessful. Throws KV exception if failure

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager.set_appname
documentation:
This are cases we need edit/remove/create confs in different app
context. call this interface to switch app context before manipulate
the confs in different app context

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager.create
splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager.update
splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager.delete
documentation: @return: exception if failure

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager.get
documentation: @return: dict object if sucess otherwise raise exception

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager.all
documentation:
@return: a dict of dict objects if success
otherwise exception

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager.set_encrypt_keys
documentation:
:keys: a list keys of a stanza which need to be encrypted
for example: ["username", "password"]

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager.is_encrypted
documentation:
:stanza: dict object
return True if the values of encrypt keys equals self.encrypted_token
otherwise return False

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager._encrypt
documentation:
:stanza: if self._keys are in stanza, encrypt the values of the key
and then mask the value to self.encrypted_token

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager._decrypt
documentation:
:stanza: if there are keys in self._keys in stanza and if the values of
the keys are self.encrypted_token, decrypt the value

methods.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.ta_conf_manager.TAConfManager._delete_creds
documentation:
:stanza: if there are keys of self._keys and the keys are in stanza,
delete the encrypted creds

methods.name: splunktalib.common.log.Logs.get_logger
documentation:
Set up a default logger.

```
param name

The log file name.

param level

The logging level.

param maxBytes

The maximum log file size before rollover.

param backupCount

The number of log files to retain.
```

methods.name: splunktalib.common.log.Logs.set_level
documentation:
Change the log level of the logging

```
param level

the level of the logging to be setLevel

param name

the name of the logging to set, in case it is not set,
all the loggers will be affected
```

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.conf_endpoints.reload_conf
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param conf_names: a list of the name of the conf file, e.g. ["props"]
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.conf_endpoints.create_stanza
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param conf_name: the name of the conf file, e.g. "props"
param key_values: the key-value dict of the stanza
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
param stanza: stanza name, e.g. "aws:cloudtrail"
return: None on success otherwise throw exception
---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.conf_endpoints.get_conf
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param conf_name: the name of the conf file, e.g. "props"
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>
param stanza: stanza name, e.g. "aws:cloudtrail"

---

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.conf_endpoints.update_stanza
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"

param session_key: splunkd session key

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.conf_endpoints.delete_stanza
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param conf_name: the name of the conf file, e.g. "props"

functions.name: splunktalib.conf_manager.conf_endpoints.operate_conf
documentation:
---
param app_name: the app"s name, e.g. "Splunk_TA_aws"
param conf_name: the name of the conf file, e.g. "props"
param operation: must be "disable" or "enable"
param owner: the owner (ACL user), e.g. "-", "nobody"
param session_key: splunkd session key
param splunkd_uri: splunkd uri, e.g. <https://127.0.0.1:8089>

---

documentation:

jobs.

methods.name: splunktalib.schedule.scheduler.Scheduler.get_ready_jobs

@return: a 2 element tuple. The first element is the next ready
         duration. The second element is ready jobs list
A simple scheduler which schedules the periodic or once event

methods.name: splunktalib.credentials.CredentialManager.set_appname
documentation:
This are cases we need edit/remove/create confs in different app
context. call this interface to switch app context before manipulate
the confs in different app context

documentation:
Get session key by using login username and passwrod
\:return: session_key if successful, None if failed

methods.name: splunktalib.credentials.CredentialManager.update
documentation:
Update or Create credentials based on the stanza
\:stanza: nested dict object. The outlayer keys are stanza name, and

> > inner dict is user/pass key/value pair to be encrypted
>
> {
> "stanza_name": {"tommy": "tommypasswod", "jerry": "jerrypassword"}
> }

```
return

raise on failure
```

methods.name: splunktalib.credentials.CredentialManager._update
documentation:
Update the string for the name.
\:return: raise on failure

documentation:

documentation: Delete the encrypted entry

documentation: Delete the encrypted entry

documentation:
---
return: a list of dict when successful, None when failed.
---

the dict at least contains
{

> "realm": xxx,
> "username": yyy,
> "clear_password": zzz,

}

methods.name: splunktalib.credentials.CredentialManager._escape_string
documentation:
Splunk secure credential storage actually requires a custom style of
escaped string where all the :'s are escaped by a single \.
But don't escape the control : in the stanza name.
Credential related interfaces

functions.name: splunktalib.modinput._parse_modinput_configs
documentation:
When user splunkd spawns modinput script to do config check or run

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<input>
  <server_host>localhost.localdomain</server_host>
  <server_uri>https://127.0.0.1:8089</server_uri>
  <session_key>xxxyyyzzz</session_key>
  <checkpoint_dir>ckpt_dir</checkpoint_dir>
  <configuration>
    <stanza name="snow://alm_asset">
      <param name="duration">60</param>
        <param name="host">localhost.localdomain</param>
        <param name="index">snow</param>
        <param name="priority">10</param>
    </stanza>
    ...
  </configuration>
</input>

When user create an stanza through data input on WebUI

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<items>
  <server_host>localhost.localdomain</server_host>
  <server_uri>https://127.0.0.1:8089</server_uri>
  <session_key>xxxyyyzzz</session_key>
  <checkpoint_dir>ckpt_dir</checkpoint_dir>
  <item name="abc">
    <param name="duration">60</param>
    <param name="exclude"></param>
    <param name="host">localhost.localdomain</param>
    <param name="index">snow</param>
    <param name="priority">10</param>
  </item>
</items>

functions.name: splunktalib.modinput.parse_modinput_configs
documentation:
@config_str: modinput XML configuration feed by splunkd
@return: meta_config and stanza_config

functions.name: splunktalib.modinput.get_modinput_configs_from_cli
documentation:
@modinput: modinput name
@modinput_stanza: modinput stanza name, for multiple instance only

functions.name: splunktalib.modinput.get_modinput_config_str_from_stdin
documentation: Get modinput from stdin which is feed by splunkd

  dict like object, contains thread_min_size (int),
  thread_max_size (int), daemonize_thread (bool),
  process_size (int)
---

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.concurrent_executor.ConcurrentExecutor.run_io_func_sync
documentation:
---
param args: free params
param func: callable
param kwargs: named params
---

\:return whatever the func returns

documentation:
---
calllback: when func is done and without exception, call the callback
param args: free params
param func: callable
param kwargs: named params
---

\:return whatever the func returns

documentation:
run jobs in a fire and forget way, no result will be handled
over to clients
\:param funcs: tuple/list-like or generator like object, func shall be

> callable

classes.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool.start
documentation: Start threads in the pool

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool.tear_down
documentation: Tear down thread pool

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool.enqueue_funcs
documentation:
run jobs in a fire and forget way, no result will be handled
over to clients
\:param funcs: tuple/list-like or generator like object, func shall be

> callable

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool.apply_async
documentation:
---
callback: when func is done and without exception, call the callback
param args: free params
param func: callable
param kwargs: named params
---

\:return AsyncResult, clients can poll or wait the result through it

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool.apply
documentation:
---
param args: free params
param func: callable
param kwargs: named params
---

\:return whatever the func returns

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool.resize
documentation: Resize the pool size, spawn or destroy threads if necessary

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool._remove_exited_threads_with_lock
documentation: Join the exited threads last time when resize was called

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.ThreadPool._run
documentation: Threads callback func, run forever to handle jobs from the job queue
A simple thread pool implementation

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.AsyncResult.get
documentation:
Return the result when it arrives. If timeout is not None and the
result does not arrive within timeout seconds then
multiprocessing.TimeoutError is raised. If the remote call raised an
exception then that exception will be reraised by get().

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.AsyncResult.wait
documentation: Wait until the result is available or until timeout seconds pass.

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.AsyncResult.ready
documentation: Return whether the call has completed.

methods.name: splunktalib.concurrent.thread_pool.AsyncResult.successful
documentation:
Return whether the call completed without raising an exception.
Will raise AssertionError if the result is not ready.
